title: Comforting a friend
setting: A friend phones you, clearly upset — they've just lost their job. You want to support them.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Ich han hüt mini Stell verlore. Ich weiss nöd, was jetz.
steps.en: I lost my job today. I don't know what now.

options.es: Oh nei, das tuet mer so leid. Wie gaht's der?
options.en: Oh no, I'm so sorry. How are you doing?
options.feedback: Acknowledges the blow and centres him. Exactly the right first move.

options.es: Du findsch sicher schnäll öppis Nöis.
options.en: You'll surely find something new quickly.
options.feedback: Well-meant, but jumping to solutions skips the comfort he needs first.
Interrogating him now feels like blame. Sit with him before the details.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Ehrlich gseit, ziemli schlächt. Es isch eifach so plötzlich cho.
steps.en: Honestly, pretty bad. It just came so suddenly.

options.es: Das verstah ich. Das isch en richtige Schock.
options.en: I understand that. That's a real shock.
options.feedback: Naming his feeling lets him feel heard. Quietly powerful.
Too brisk for raw news — let him feel it before you cheer him on.

options.en: You should have been more careful.
options.feedback: Criticism is the last thing he needs right now. Drop it entirely.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Merci, dass du zueloosch. Das hilft scho.
steps.en: Thanks for listening. That already helps.

options.es: Immer. Wottsch verbiicho? Mir chönd zäme znacht ässe.
options.en: Always. Want to come over? We could have dinner together.
options.feedback: Turns support into a concrete offer of company. Genuinely kind.

options.en: Of course, that's what friends are for.
options.feedback: Warm and reassuring — reminds him he's not alone.
A bit quick to end — he reached out, so stay with him a moment longer.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Das tät mer guet. Ich chume verbii.
steps.en: That would do me good. I'll come over.

options.es: Super. Mir finded zäme en Wäg, versproche.
options.en: Great. We'll find a way through together, I promise.
options.feedback: Reassuring without false promises about the job itself. Perfect note.
Casual, but the moment calls for warmth over logistics.

options.es: Aber nöd z'lang blibe, ich bi müed.
options.en: But don't stay too long, I'm tired.
options.feedback: Setting limits now undercuts the whole offer. Be fully there.
